financial.note: Holds ~23.3% stake; the visionary leader driving the 'WHA: SHAPE THE FUTURE' 2026 mission.
Holds ~7.5% stake; reflects significant foreign institutional sentiment toward Thailand's industrial and digital infrastructure.
Major providers of the ฿16.5bn 2026 investment budget; sensitive to interest rate cuts (expected to 1.0% in 1H26).

supply_chain.note: The primary growth driver in 2026; data center water consumption is 12–16x higher than standard manufacturing, fueling WHAUP revenue.
WHA is the main beneficiary of the EV supply chain 'reshoring' to Thailand's EEC; land sales target for 2026 is 2,500 rai.
Provides vertical integration; WHAUP secured PPAs for 135.8 MW solar projects in April 2026 to power the 'Green Economy'.

equity_cross_holdings.note: WHA plans to divest 193,000 sq.m. of assets (~฿4.7bn) into WHART in late 2026 to recycle capital.
Targeting 637 cumulative EV rental vehicles in 2026 as part of the green logistics push.

competitors.note: Direct rival for FDI in industrial estates (Thailand and Vietnam); AMATA is a major competitor for the same high-tech 'S-Curve' clients.
Competes for electronics and automotive tenants in central and eastern Thailand.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Record 2025 BOI applications (฿1.88tn) act as a leading indicator for WHA's 2026 land sales and transfers.
Influences FDI attractiveness; a weaker THB makes Thai industrial assets cheaper for foreign tech and EV giants.
Impacts the profitability of WHAUP's power business and the operational costs of large-scale data center tenants.
